title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: rush network consists multiple workers communicate via shared Redis database. worker evaluates tasks pushes corresponding results back database, illustrated Figure 1. Figure 1: communication flow worker Redis database rush network. octagon represents worker rectangle represents Redis database. worker W runs instance optimizer \(\mathcal{O}\), evaluates objective function \(f\), exchanges task information via shared Redis database. Arrows indicate flow information: workers retrieve completed tasks via $fetch_finished_tasks(), propose store new tasks via $push_running_tasks(), report results via $finish_tasks().

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: begin simple random search illustrate core concepts rush. Although random search require communication workers, introduces worker loop, tasks, manager. use Branin function \(f\) optimization target: \[f(x_1,x_2)=\left(x_2-\frac{5.1}{4\pi^2}x_1^2+\frac{5}{\pi}x_1-6\right)^2 +10\left(1-\frac{1}{8\pi}\right)\cos(x_1)+10\] function optimized domain \(x_1 \[-5, 10]\) \(x_2 \[0, 15]\). commonly used benchmark function fast evaluate yet sufficiently nontrivial.  Branin function define worker_loop function, executed worker. function repeatedly samples random point, evaluates using Branin function, writes result Redis database. takes RushWorker object (rush) objective function branin arguments. loop terminates 100 tasks evaluated. worker loop relies two principal methods. $push_running_tasks() method creates new task database, marks "running", returns unique key identifying . $finish_tasks() method takes key along result writes database, marking task "finished". $n_finished_tasks field tracks number completed tasks serves termination criterion. Marking task "running" evaluation essential random search, important algorithms use states workers’ tasks inform next proposal. Tasks basic units workers exchange information. task consists four components: unique key, computational state, input (xs), output (ys). input output lists may contain arbitrary data. Tasks pass one four computational states: "running", "finished", "failed", "queued". $push_running_tasks() method creates tasks marked "running" returns keys. Upon completion, $finish_tasks() marks tasks "finished" stores associated results. Tasks encounter errors can marked "failed" using $fail_tasks() method. fourth state, "queued", supports queue mechanism described Section 4.1. Rush manager class responsible starting, monitoring, stopping workers within network. initialized using rsh() function, requires network identifier config argument. config argument specifies configuration file used connect Redis database via redux package. Workers started using $start_workers() method accepts worker loop number workers arguments. additional named arguments forwarded worker loop function. workers run mirai daemons started mirai::daemons() function. optimization completes, results can retrieved database. $fetch_finished_tasks() method returns data.table containing task key, input, result. worker_id column identifies worker evaluated task. auxiliary information can passed $push_running_tasks() $finish_tasks() via extra argument. Printing rush object displays number running workers number tasks state. Note total tasks slightly exceeds 100 workers check stopping condition independently: multiple workers evaluate condition concurrently — example, 99 tasks finished — may create new task detecting limit reached. workers can stopped database reset using $reset() method.

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: define worker_loop function, executed worker. function repeatedly samples random point, evaluates using Branin function, writes result Redis database. takes RushWorker object (rush) objective function branin arguments. loop terminates 100 tasks evaluated. worker loop relies two principal methods. $push_running_tasks() method creates new task database, marks "running", returns unique key identifying . $finish_tasks() method takes key along result writes database, marking task "finished". $n_finished_tasks field tracks number completed tasks serves termination criterion. Marking task "running" evaluation essential random search, important algorithms use states workers’ tasks inform next proposal.
code: library(rush)  wl_random_search = function(rush, branin) {   while (rush$n_finished_tasks < 100) {      xs = list(x1 = runif(1, -5, 10), x2 = runif(1, 0, 15))     key = rush$push_running_tasks(xss = list(xs))      ys = list(y = branin(xs$x1, xs$x2))     rush$finish_tasks(key, yss = list(ys))   } }

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: Tasks basic units workers exchange information. task consists four components: unique key, computational state, input (xs), output (ys). input output lists may contain arbitrary data. Tasks pass one four computational states: "running", "finished", "failed", "queued". $push_running_tasks() method creates tasks marked "running" returns keys. Upon completion, $finish_tasks() marks tasks "finished" stores associated results. Tasks encounter errors can marked "failed" using $fail_tasks() method. fourth state, "queued", supports queue mechanism described Section 4.1.

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: Rush manager class responsible starting, monitoring, stopping workers within network. initialized using rsh() function, requires network identifier config argument. config argument specifies configuration file used connect Redis database via redux package. Workers started using $start_workers() method accepts worker loop number workers arguments. additional named arguments forwarded worker loop function. workers run mirai daemons started mirai::daemons() function. optimization completes, results can retrieved database. $fetch_finished_tasks() method returns data.table containing task key, input, result. worker_id column identifies worker evaluated task. auxiliary information can passed $push_running_tasks() $finish_tasks() via extra argument. Printing rush object displays number running workers number tasks state. Note total tasks slightly exceeds 100 workers check stopping condition independently: multiple workers evaluate condition concurrently — example, 99 tasks finished — may create new task detecting limit reached. workers can stopped database reset using $reset() method.

code: wl_median_stopping = function(rush, training_ids, test_ids, mtcars_data, response) {   while (rush$n_finished_tasks < 1000) {     params = list(       max_depth = sample(1:20, 1),       lambda = runif(1, 0, 1),       alpha = runif(1, 0, 1)     )      model = NULL     for (iteration in seq(5, 20)) {        key = rush$push_running_tasks(xss = list(c(params, list(nrounds = iteration))))        model = xgboost::xgboost(         data = as.matrix(mtcars_data[training_ids, ]),         label = response[training_ids],         nrounds = if (is.null(model)) 5 else 1,         params = params,         xgb_model = model,         verbose = 0       )        pred = predict(model, as.matrix(mtcars_data[test_ids, ]))       rmse = sqrt(mean((pred - response[test_ids])^2))        rush$finish_tasks(key, yss = list(list(rmse = rmse)))        tasks = rush$fetch_finished_tasks()       ref = tasks[nrounds == iteration, rmse]       if (length(ref) > 0 && rmse > median(ref)) break     }   } } data(mtcars)  training_ids = sample(seq_len(nrow(mtcars)), 20) test_ids = setdiff(seq_len(nrow(mtcars)), training_ids) mtcars_data = mtcars[, -1] response = mtcars$mpg  config = redux::redis_config()  rush = rsh(   network = "example-median-stopping",   config = config)  mirai::daemons(4)  rush$start_workers(   worker_loop = wl_median_stopping,   n_workers = 4,   training_ids = training_ids,   test_ids = test_ids,   mtcars_data = mtcars_data,   response = response) rush$fetch_finished_tasks()[order(rmse)] worker_id max_depth    lambda     alpha nrounds     rmse          keys            <char>     <int>     <num>     <num>   <int>    <num>        <char>  1: gentlewoma...        14 0.1341924 0.3968682       5 3.278504 c3bda5e9-b...  2: subcultura...        17 0.9892150 0.3596648       5 3.278504 885c8e99-4...  3: aurous_tat...         4 0.6366683 0.6386029       5 3.278504 448adb33-c...  4: vanilla_pe...         9 0.8841333 0.0233838       5 3.278504 1b6925c7-4...  5: gentlewoma...        14 0.1341924 0.3968682       6 5.772041 17e68f24-0...  6: subcultura...        17 0.9892150 0.3596648       6 5.772041 4046b1ee-3...  7: gentlewoma...        14 0.1341924 0.3968682       7 5.772041 f7020880-a...  8: aurous_tat...         4 0.6366683 0.6386029       6 5.772041 56afcfba-7...  9: subcultura...        17 0.9892150 0.3596648       7 5.772041 31443013-6... 10: gentlewoma...        14 0.1341924 0.3968682       8 5.772041 15944c02-2... 11: aurous_tat...         4 0.6366683 0.6386029       7 5.772041 70d0410b-5... 12: subcultura...        17 0.9892150 0.3596648       8 5.772041 fc880cd2-3... rush$reset()

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: implement Asynchronous Decentralized Bayesian Optimization (ADBO) (Egelé et al. 2023) demonstrates use shared task information queue mechanism. ADBO runs sequential Bayesian optimization multiple workers parallel. worker maintains surrogate model independently proposes next configuration maximizing upper confidence bound acquisition function. promote varying exploration–exploitation trade-offs across workers, \(\lambda\) parameter acquisition function sampled independently worker. worker completes evaluation, shares result via database; workers incorporate information local surrogate models next iteration. typical task lifecycle rush running finished, package also supports queue mechanism cases tasks created centrally distributed workers. initialize rush network push initial Latin hypercube sampling (LHS) design queue. Structured designs LHS can outperform random designs, generating requires global view design space. queue avoids redundant evaluations: design generated main process, workers draw tasks shared queue. worker loop first drains initial design queue using $pop_task() method, retrieves next queued task, marks "running", returns . queue empty, $pop_task() returns NULL, signaling transition model-based optimization phase. $fetch_tasks_with_state() method retrieves tasks specified states database, returning data.table containing task states, keys, inputs, results. Using $fetch_tasks_with_state() rather separate calls $fetch_running_tasks() $fetch_finished_tasks() prevents tasks appearing twice state transition occurs fetch. start four workers wait optimization complete.
code: config = redux::redis_config()  rush = rsh(   network = "example-bayesian-optimization",   config = config) lhs_points = lhs::maximinLHS(n = 25, k = 2) x1_lower = -5 x1_range = 15 x2_lower = 0 x2_range = 15  xss = lapply(1:25, function(i) {   # rescale to the domain   list(x1 = lhs_points[i, 1] * x1_range + x1_lower, x2 = lhs_points[i, 2] * x2_range + x2_lower) })  rush$push_tasks(xss = xss)  rush ── <Rush> ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── • Running Workers: 0 • Queued Tasks: 25 • Running Tasks: 0 • Finished Tasks: 0 • Failed Tasks: 0 wl_bayesian_optimization = function(rush, branin) {   repeat {     task = rush$pop_task()     if (is.null(task)) break     ys = list(y = branin(task$xs$x1, task$xs$x2))     rush$finish_tasks(task$key, yss = list(ys))   }    lambda = runif(1, 0.01, 10)    while (rush$n_finished_tasks < 100) {      archive = rush$fetch_tasks_with_state(states = c("running", "finished"))     mean_y = mean(archive$y, na.rm = TRUE)     archive["running", y := mean_y, on = "state"]      surrogate = ranger::ranger(       y ~ x1 + x2,       data = archive,       num.trees = 100L,       keep.inbag = TRUE)      xdt = data.table::data.table(x1 = runif(1000, -5, 10), x2 = runif(1000, 0, 15))     p = predict(surrogate, xdt, type = "se", se.method = "jack")     cb = p$predictions - lambda * p$se     xs = as.list(xdt[which.min(cb)])      key = rush$push_running_tasks(xss = list(xs))     ys = list(y = branin(xs$x1, xs$x2))     rush$finish_tasks(key, yss = list(ys))   } } mirai::daemons(4)  rush$start_workers(   worker_loop = wl_bayesian_optimization,   n_workers = 4,   branin = branin) rush$fetch_finished_tasks()[order(y)] worker_id        x1         x2          y          keys             <char>     <num>      <num>      <num>        <char>   1: hoplophobi...  9.873379  2.3595010   1.618269 7c00ea31-d...   2: amphisbaen...  9.446329  3.9391370   2.490738 9caaad4e-a...   3: cohesive_a... -3.529241 14.4592488   2.631202 d4c7b296-a...   4: amphisbaen...  3.348502  0.6250960   2.834954 4edf465b-2...   5: cohesive_a... -3.740751 14.6821367   2.918419 e7378e1e-9...  ---  99: unromantic... -4.708241  0.6111365 257.895582 534d0f0c-0... 100: cohesive_a... -4.777719  0.2162933 277.508938 f6fdb9c7-d... 101: cohesive_a... -4.787689  0.2266244 278.188127 a175a3ee-0... 102: amphisbaen... -4.994237  0.5143975 290.104663 f0f77c32-7... 103: hoplophobi... -4.961248  0.2867761 294.236824 849cd7b3-7...

title: Code review: rush
text: rush package , whole, well-engineered Redis-backed distributed task engine: crash-recovery primitives (BLMOVE--per-worker-pending-list, eviction-tolerant fetch paths, monotonic-counter gating wait_for_tasks()) thoughtfully designed well commented, missing-hash regression tests directly target hardening described NEWS. dominant risk area lost-worker recovery path: three distinct non-atomic Redis sequences (.fail_lost_tasks using stale running_tasks snapshot, pop_task’s unconditional SADD running_tasks, un-transactional state move finish_tasks/fail_tasks) can leave task two terminal states lose entirely heartbeat false-positive. None fire deterministically — require narrow timing window plus (mostly) opt-heartbeats — must-fix correctness defects rather outright blockers. separate empty_queue()--empty-queue bug permanently leaks unnamespaced Redis hash even $reset() reclaim (reproduced live review). Beyond , findings resource-cleanup gaps, genuinely broken public signature (push_failed_tasks), long tail consistency/style/documentation nits. blocking defects, several required fixes ship.

title: Code review: rush
text: .fail_lost_tasks double-states task worker already finished — R/Rush.R:1728-1756 — running_tasks snapshotted top detect_lost_workers() (line 572) passed several Redis round-trips. worker finishes task (SREM running_tasks + RPUSH finished_tasks) snapshot flagged lost still stale snapshot, .fail_lost_tasks runs unconditional SREM running_tasks + SADD failed_tasks inside MULTI/EXEC. SREM -op SADD corrupts state: key lands finished_tasks (list) failed_tasks (set), fetch_tasks_with_state() returns twice n_finished_tasks + n_failed_tasks double-counts. Commit ee39c36 introduced replacing guarded SMOVE (-op key already left running_tasks) unconditional SREM+SADD. Fix: gate move task still running — use SMOVE running_tasks failed_tasks key (atomic, moves present), SADD keys whose SREM returned 1, check SMISMEMBER finished_tasks inside transaction. Important: apply gate keys sourced running_tasks snapshot. Keys recovered per-worker pending_task list (line 1732) popped queue never added running_tasks, SMOVE/SREM-returned-1 guard make -ops task lost — keys must still failed unconditionally. fix must distinguish two key sources, blanket-gate . pop_task unconditionally re-adds recovered task running_tasks — R/RushWorker.R:132-150 — pending→running transition split across BLMOVE, separate write_hashes, separate MULTI { LPOP pending; SADD running_tasks key }. detect_lost_workers() (via heartbeat false-positive path) recovers key pending list — SADD failed_tasks key, DEL pending — window MULTI, worker’s LPOP becomes -op SADD running_tasks key still runs (MULTI gives conditional logic; SADD depend LPOP result). still-alive worker evaluates task calls finish_tasks, leaving key failed_tasks finished_tasks simultaneously — double-processed double-counted. Fix: make running transition conditional pop. Use Lua EVAL LPOPs pending SADDs running_tasks pop returned expected key (key already failed_tasks); pending empty, return NULL worker skips already-recovered task. finish_tasks/fail_tasks use non-transactional pipeline state move — silent task loss mid-move crash — R/RushWorker.R:212-217 (identical fail_tasks, 242-252) — r$pipeline(SREM running_tasks; RPUSH finished_tasks) batches one round-trip transaction. worker killed connection drops SREM applied server-side RPUSH delivered, key removed running_tasks never appears finished_tasks. Recovery reclaim : .fail_lost_tasks joins running_tasks pending list, key now neither, task silently permanently lost (wait_for_tasks can hang worker survives transient loss). Notably .fail_lost_tasks (R/Rush.R:1751) wraps SREM+SADD move MULTI/EXEC, unwrapped version inconsistency signals oversight. Fix: wrap move MULTI/EXEC like recovery path: r$pipeline(.commands = c(list("MULTI"), list(c("SREM", ...), c("RPUSH", ...)), list("EXEC"))). Apply methods. empty_queue() empty queue leaks permanently-orphaned, unnamespaced hash — R/Rush.R:887-918 — queue empty, LRANGE returns nothing keys NULL. write_hashes(condition = conditions, keys = NULL) takes auto-generate branch (n_hashes = max(lens) = 1, keys = UUIDgenerate(n = 1)) writes default condition brand-new bare-UUID hash network_id prefix. outer keys stays NULL (write_hashes return captured) subsequent SADD loop -op; hash registered list/set , unnamespaced, never matches network_id:*, $reset() every cleanup path miss . Reproduced live review: rush$empty_queue() empty queue raised DBSIZE 0 1, creating bare bf37a267-… hash still EXISTS rush$reset(). Reachable directly via deprecated $fail_tasks(), calls empty_queue() unconditionally. Fix: add (!length(keys)) return(invisible(self)) immediately LRANGE/DEL, write_hashes. push_failed_tasks required argument defaulted one — unusable positionally contradicts docs — R/Rush.R:860 — signature function(xss, xss_extra = NULL, conditions). natural call push_failed_tasks(xss, conditions) binds second arg xss_extra, leaving conditions unbound, errors argument "conditions" missing, default. Every sibling push_*/finish_* method takes mandatory payload second positional arg, class-level roxygen (line 20) even advertises $push_failed_tasks(xss, conditions) — broken positional form. Fix: reorder function(xss, conditions, xss_extra = NULL), update @param order, add NEWS bullet (public-signature change). Heartbeat process heartbeat key never stopped normal worker termination — R/RushWorker.R:263-273 — set_terminated() SMOVEs worker running_worker_ids terminated_worker_ids. push kill key, DEL heartbeat key, SREM heartbeat_keys. heartbeat callr::r_bg(..., supervise = TRUE) process reaped worker R process exits — default mirai backend reuses long-lived daemon, one start_worker call returns daemon stays heartbeat process keeps EXPIRE-ing key forever. title: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: Package website: release | dev rush R package asynchronous decentralized optimization. uses database-centric architecture workers communicate shared Redis database. support high-throughput workloads, rush combines sub-millisecond per-task overhead caching strategies reduce database operations. package integrates mlr3 ecosystem serves backend asynchronous optimization algorithms bbotk mlr3tuning.

title: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: Database-centric architecture decentralized optimization without central controller. Asynchronous communication workers using Redis. Sub-millisecond per-task overhead high-throughput workloads. Efficient caching mechanism minimizes database read/write operations. Task queue support centralized tasks distribution needed. Fast data transformation Redis data.table. Scales large remote worker networks via mirai package. Worker logging directly Redis database using lgr. Minimal dependencies lightweight integration.

title: Rush — Rush
text: Tasks unit workers exchange information. main components task key, computational state, input (xs), output (ys). key unique identifier task Redis database. four possible computational states "running", "finished", "failed", "queued". input xs output ys lists can contain arbitrary data. Methods create task: $push_running_tasks(xss): Create running tasks $push_finished_tasks(xss, yss): Create finished tasks. $push_failed_tasks(xss, conditions): Create failed tasks. $push_tasks(xss): Create queued tasks. methods return key created tasks. methods work multiple tasks , xss yss lists inputs outputs. Methods change state existing task: $finish_tasks(keys, yss): Save output tasks mark finished. $fail_tasks(keys, conditions): Mark tasks failed optionally save condition objects. $pop_task(): Pop task queue mark running. methods $pop_task(), $push_running_tasks(xss), $finish_tasks(keys, yss), $fail_tasks(keys, conditions) available RushWorker. following methods used fetch tasks: $fetch_tasks(): Fetch tasks. $fetch_finished_tasks(): Fetch finished tasks. $fetch_failed_tasks(): Fetch failed tasks. $fetch_tasks_with_state(): Fetch tasks different states . $fetch_new_tasks(): Fetch new tasks optionally block new tasks available. methods return data.table() tasks. Tasks following fields: xs: input task. ys: output task. xs_extra: Metadata created creating task. ys_extra: Metadata created finishing task. condition: Condition object task failed. worker_id: id worker created task.

what: rush: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing — rush-package
title: rush: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing — rush-package
text: Package tackle large-scale problems asynchronously across distributed network. Employing database centric model, rush enables workers communicate tasks results shared 'Redis' database. Key features include low task overhead, efficient caching, robust error handling. package powers asynchronous optimization algorithms 'bbotk' 'mlr3tuning' packages.

title: rush (development version)
text: $fail_tasks() $finish_tasks() moved Rush RushWorker task marked failed finished worker processes . $fail_tasks() $finish_tasks() now move task states atomically MULTI/EXEC worker crash mid-move can longer remove task running state without recording failed finished. deprecated methods $push_failed() $push_results() removed. Use $fail_tasks() $finish_tasks() instead. $empty_queue() longer creates orphaned hash Redis database queue already empty. $detect_lost_workers() longer marks task finished failed, loses task, worker finishes task starts new one detection begins worker flagged lost. running tasks lost worker now read worker flagged lost, running task moved failed state SMOVE, -op task already left running state. $pop_task() longer permanently loses task worker crashes popping task queue marking running. task now moved atomically per-worker pending list recovered failed task $detect_lost_workers(). $fetch_finished_tasks() $fetch_new_tasks() longer crash return duplicated results finished task hashes removed database. cache now tracks consumed entries finished tasks list separately cached rows. $fetch_tasks() related methods longer fail task hashes removed database. Affected tasks dropped warning. $start_local_workers() longer generates unparseable worker startup code Windows temporary directory path contains quotes. temporary arguments file now deleted worker reads . $wait_for_tasks() longer reads full finished failed task structures every poll. expensive membership check now runs cheap completion counters report newly completed task. $worker_script() longer logs Redis password. logged script shows <redacted> instead. method now returns script visibly. RushWorker$new() now errors heartbeat process fails set heartbeat key within startup timeout instead silently registering dead heartbeat. heartbeat_period heartbeat_expire must now least 1 second. $pop_task() $push_running_tasks() moved Rush RushWorker require worker identity. Calling Rush manager previously stored empty worker_id task hashes. $reset() now also resets internal log counter, $print_log() longer skips messages network id reused reset. rush_plan() gains start_worker_timeout argument, sets default timeout used $wait_for_workers(). explicit timeout passed $wait_for_workers() longer overridden configuration. start_worker() longer errors exit message_log output_log set. sinks now reverted log connections closed.

title: rush 1.1.0

text: CRAN release: 2026-03-18 feat: Add $push_finished_tasks() method. feat: Combine $wait_for_new_tasks() $fetch_new_tasks() $fetch_new_tasks() timeout argument. refactor: $wait_for_finished_tasks() method removed. perf: Cache now data.table() instead list. data_format argument removed. refactor: Seed mechanism removed.
